name: Peaks
description: Dual trigger-processor. Four main functions (ADSR envelope, LFO, tap-tempo LFO, drum synth) plus four hidden (mini sequencer, trigger delay/repeat, probabilistic stream, digital drum). Twin/split/expert routing across two channels.

controls.name: SPLIT button
controls.description: Short press toggles twin (both channels share knobs) and split (knobs 1/2 = CH1, knobs 3/4 = CH2). Hold 1s: enter expert mode, button then swaps which channel the knobs edit.
controls.detail: LED off: twin · LED on: split · blinking: expert (hold to exit)

controls.name: FUNCTION button
controls.description: Short press cycles the four main functions: envelope, LFO, tap LFO, drum. Hold 1s (blinks): switch to the four hidden alternative functions.
controls.detail: 4 main × 2 alt banks = 8 modes · hold again to return to main

controls.name: Knob 1
controls.scope: ENV twin: Attack · ENV split CH1: Attack · LFO twin: Frequency · DRUM twin: Base freq
controls.description: First parameter for the active mode. Meaning follows the function/mode table.
controls.detail: mapping depends on FUNCTION × SPLIT state

controls.name: Knob 2
controls.scope: ENV twin: Decay · ENV split CH1: Decay · LFO twin: Waveform · DRUM twin: Punch/Tone
controls.description: Second parameter for the active mode.
controls.detail: mapping depends on FUNCTION × SPLIT state

controls.name: Knob 3
controls.scope: ENV twin: Sustain · ENV split CH2: Attack · LFO twin: Waveform variation · DRUM twin: Tone/Snappy
controls.description: Third parameter for the active mode.
controls.detail: mapping depends on FUNCTION × SPLIT state

controls.name: Knob 4
controls.scope: ENV twin: Release · ENV split CH2: Decay · LFO twin: Phase · DRUM twin: Decay
controls.description: Fourth parameter for the active mode.
controls.detail: mapping depends on FUNCTION × SPLIT state

controls.name: TRIG 1 switch
controls.description: Momentary trigger button for CH 1. LED shows CH 1 output activity. Overrides TRIG 1 jack while pressed.
controls.detail: tactile switch · LED = output level indicator

controls.name: TRIG 2 switch
controls.description: Momentary trigger button for CH 2. LED shows CH 2 output activity.
controls.detail: tactile switch · LED = output level indicator

inputs.name: TRIG 1
inputs.description: Gate/trigger input for channel 1. Starts envelope, resets LFO, learns tap tempo, or fires drum depending on mode.

inputs.name: TRIG 2
inputs.description: Gate/trigger input for channel 2. Same function as TRIG 1 on CH 2. Also resets CH 1 in the alternative mini-sequencer (twin mode).

outputs.name: OUT 1
outputs.description: Channel 1 output. Envelope, LFO, tap LFO, bass drum (main banks) or sequencer/trigger-delay/probabilistic/909-kick (alt bank).
outputs.voltage: 0 – +8V CV · ±5V LFO · audio level for drums

outputs.name: OUT 2
outputs.description: Channel 2 output. Same per-mode behaviour as OUT 1, except the main drum bank is snare/hi-hat (CH2) rather than bass drum (CH1).
outputs.voltage: 0 – +8V CV · ±5V LFO · audio level for drums

behaviors.name: Twin mode (knobs shared)
behaviors.description: Both channels share all four knobs — full parameter set exposed. CH 1 and CH 2 have identical settings, each responding to its own TRIG input. Use for duophonic voices or two phase-shifted copies of the same LFO/envelope.

behaviors.name: Split mode (2+2 knobs)
behaviors.description: Knobs 1 & 2 control CH 1's two most essential params; knobs 3 & 4 control CH 2's. The remaining parameters snap to 'neutral' defaults. Ideal for quick dual-AD envelopes, dual simple LFOs, or a preset 808 kick + snare voicing.

behaviors.name: Expert mode (per-channel)
behaviors.description: Knobs edit one channel at a time; the SPLIT button toggles which channel the knobs control. Gives full 4-parameter access per channel asymmetrically. Hold SPLIT again to exit.

behaviors.name: ENV function: ADSR/AD
behaviors.description: Twin/expert = full ADSR (A, D, S, R on knobs 1-4). Split = dual AD envelope (A/D for CH1, A/D for CH2). TRIG input starts and holds the envelope: ADSR requires a sustained gate; AD fires on trigger edges.

behaviors.name: LFO function with waveform variation
behaviors.description: Five waveforms: sine, linear slope, square, steps, random. Knob 3 (twin) morphs within: wavefolder for sine, asc/tri/desc balance for slope, PWM for square, number of steps for steps, interpolation for random. TRIG resets the waveform to the phase set by knob 4.

behaviors.name: Tap-LFO function
behaviors.trigger: FUNCTION = tap-LFO · TRIG pulses set period
behaviors.description: Tap-tempo LFO: the TRIG input sets the LFO period (one full cycle per tap interval). Peaks tracks irregular trigger streams and locks onto their average — drop in a swung clock and the LFO follows. Knob 1 is amplitude; knobs 2-4 mirror the regular LFO's waveform controls.

behaviors.name: Drum function (BD / SD+HH)
behaviors.description: CH 1 is a bass-drum voice (knobs: base freq, punch, tone, decay). CH 2 is a snare/hi-hat voice (knobs: freq, tone, snappy, decay). Split mode gives a fixed '808 sweet-spot' voicing with BD tone + decay and SD tone + snappy on the four knobs.

behaviors.name: Alt bank: mini-sequencer
behaviors.description: Twin: 4-step mini-sequencer on CH 1 (clocked by TRIG 1, reset by TRIG 2); CH 2 runs independently clocked by TRIG 2. Split: dual 2-step sequencer, no reset, per-channel clocking. Each knob is a step's output voltage.

behaviors.name: Alt bank: trigger delay/repeater
behaviors.description: Incoming triggers are delayed with pre-delay, output gate duration, delay, and number of repeats (twin). Split reduces to delay + repeats per channel. Generates rolls, flams, and metric delays without an external module.

behaviors.name: Alt bank: trigger randomizer
behaviors.description: Probabilistic trigger processor. Knob 1 = probability trigger is accepted. Knob 2 = probability it is regenerated after delay. Knob 3 = delay time. Knob 4 = jitter. Converts a steady clock into probabilistic, slightly swung trigger streams.

behaviors.name: Alt bank: digital drum synth
behaviors.description: Anushri-style FM drum synth with frequency, FM intensity, FM/AM decay, and colour (center = clean, left = 909 distortion, right = snare noise). Split mode offers preset morphs between BD (CH1) and SD (CH2) variations.

behaviors.name: Firmware-mod TRIG threshold
behaviors.description: Peaks enters audio-bootloader mode when powered on with TRIG 1 held. Play the firmware WAV into TRIG 1 input with monitors muted — the three LEDs blink on each valid block. Used for official updates and Tim Churches' Mutated-Mutables community firmware (extra wavetables, extended ranges).

patchIdeas.name: Dual ADSR voice
patchIdeas.patch: FUNCTION: envelope · SPLIT off (twin) · knobs 1-4 set A/D/S/R · sequencer gate → TRIG 1 (for VCF env) and TRIG 2 (for VCA env) · OUT 1 → filter cutoff · OUT 2 → VCA CV · one Peaks covers both modulation envelopes for a classic voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: envelope
* SPLIT: off (twin ADSR)

Sequencer:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> Peaks (TRIG 1)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Peaks (TRIG 2)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)
- Peaks (OUT 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Peaks (OUT 2) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature LFO from twin reset
patchIdeas.patch: FUNCTION: LFO · SPLIT off · knob 4 (phase on restart) set to 90° offset · TRIG 1 and TRIG 2 both reset from the same clock · OUT 1 and OUT 2 are phase-shifted copies of the same sine wave · pan modulation and stereo filter motion from one module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: LFO
* Waveform: sine
* Phase: 90 degrees

Clock:
Filter:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> Peaks (TRIG 1)
- Clock (Out) c> Peaks (TRIG 2)
- Peaks (OUT 1) >> Filter (Cutoff L)
- Peaks (OUT 2) >> Filter (Cutoff R) // 90 deg offset

patchIdeas.name: Tap-tempo LFO follows groove
patchIdeas.patch: FUNCTION: tap-LFO · drummer or clocked gate (swung or irregular) → TRIG 1 · Peaks locks to the average period · OUT 1 modulates delay time for tempo-synced dub delay that follows the groove even when it changes · amplitude knob sets modulation depth.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: tap-LFO
* Waveform: sine
* Amplitude: 3 o'clock

Clock:
Delay:

- Clock (Out) c> Peaks (TRIG 1) // swung clock learned
- Peaks (OUT 1) >> Delay (Time CV)

patchIdeas.name: 808 kick + snare from one module
patchIdeas.patch: FUNCTION: drum · SPLIT on · knobs 1/2 = BD tone/decay, knobs 3/4 = SD tone/snappy · sequencer pattern 1 → TRIG 1 (kick) · pattern 2 → TRIG 2 (snare) · OUT 1 + OUT 2 → mixer · instant '808 sweet-spot' drum kit in 8HP.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: drum
* SPLIT: on (808 sweet-spot)

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pattern 1) t> Peaks (TRIG 1) // kick
- Sequencer (Pattern 2) t> Peaks (TRIG 2) // snare
- Peaks (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Peaks (OUT 2) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: 4-step mini sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: Hold FUNCTION → alt ENV (mini-seq) · SPLIT off · clock → TRIG 1 (advance) · reset gate → TRIG 2 (reset to step 1) · knobs 1-4 set each step's voltage · OUT 1 → quantizer → VCO V/Oct for a 4-note melody with per-knob tuning · CH 2 runs its own 4-step loop on TRIG 2.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: alt-envelope (mini-seq)
* SPLIT: off

Clock:
Reset:
Quantizer:
VCO:

- Clock (Out) c> Peaks (TRIG 1)
- Reset (Gate) g> Peaks (TRIG 2) // resets CH 1
- Peaks (OUT 1) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Trigger repeater rolls
patchIdeas.patch: Hold FUNCTION → alt LFO (trigger delay) · SPLIT off · kick trigger → TRIG 1 · knob 2 sets gate duration · knob 3 sets delay · knob 4 sets repeat count · OUT 1 → kick drum → one hit becomes a roll of 3-6 evenly-spaced retriggers · knob 1 adds a pre-delay for flam-style offsets.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: alt-LFO (trigger delay)
* Repeats: 4

Sequencer:
Drum:

- Sequencer (Kick Trig) t> Peaks (TRIG 1)
- Peaks (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // roll of 4 repeats

patchIdeas.name: Probabilistic hi-hat
patchIdeas.patch: Hold FUNCTION → alt tap-LFO (randomizer) · SPLIT on · clock → TRIG 1 · knob 1 = acceptance probability (~60%) · knob 2 = regeneration probability · OUT 1 → hi-hat trigger · produces a classic 'randomly dropping' hat pattern without an external probabilistic gate.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: alt-tap (randomizer)
* SPLIT: on
* Accept probability: ~60%

Clock:
HiHat:

- Clock (Out) c> Peaks (TRIG 1)
- Peaks (OUT 1) t> HiHat (Trig) // ~60% of clocks pass

patchIdeas.name: Digital FM drum voice
patchIdeas.patch: Hold FUNCTION → alt drum (digital synth) · SPLIT off · trigger → TRIG 1 · knob 1 = frequency (tune for tom/kick/metallic) · knob 2 = FM intensity · knob 3 = decay · knob 4 = colour (CCW into 909 distortion, CW into noise snare) · OUT 1 → mixer for a tunable FM/noise drum voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: alt-drum (digital)
* SPLIT: off
* Color: full CCW (909 distortion)

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Kick Trig) t> Peaks (TRIG 1)
- Peaks (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Hands-free LFO morph
patchIdeas.patch: FUNCTION: LFO · SPLIT on · knob 2 (CH 1 waveform) sweeps from sine → slope → square → steps → random · LFO from an external modulator → TRIG 1 at slow rate · Peaks changes wave shape on each reset edge · produces evolving modulation without touching the knobs.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: LFO
* SPLIT: on
* Knob 2: auto-swept externally

LFO Ext:
Filter:

- LFO Ext (Slow) c> Peaks (TRIG 1) // resets + steps morph
- Peaks (OUT 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Twin drum layering
patchIdeas.patch: FUNCTION: drum · SPLIT off (twin) · both channels run identical BD/SD voicing on knobs · sequencer → TRIG 1 only · CH 1 = BD at OUT 1 · CH 2 = SD at OUT 2 · feed same trigger to both to get a punchy combined hit (layered kick + snare accent) · tune knobs for a unique hybrid drum.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* FUNCTION: drum
* SPLIT: off (twin)

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Trig) t> Peaks (TRIG 1)
- Sequencer (Trig) t> Peaks (TRIG 2) // simultaneous layer
- Peaks (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In 1) // kick layer
- Peaks (OUT 2) -> Mixer (In 2) // snare layer

patchIdeas.name: Mutated-Mutables firmware
patchIdeas.patch: Install Tim Churches' Mutated-Mutables firmware via audio bootloader (TRIG 1 held at power-on, play WAV into TRIG 1 with monitors muted). Adds extra LFO wavetables, longer envelope times, reworked drum presets · same UI, expanded behaviour · no hardware change needed.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Peaks:
* Firmware: Mutated-Mutables (community)

Computer:

- Computer (Audio Out) -> Peaks (TRIG 1) // bootloader mode
